name: F G Goldbach Bus Co
districts_or_customers: likely Cape Henlopen or Indian River school district (Sussex County, DE) - not verified
recent_news: No public news found. Delaware uses small family-owned bus contractors widely (delbus.org members); F G Goldbach not currently visible on Delaware School Bus Contractors Assn member directory or Cape Henlopen/Indian River contractor lists via search.
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-operator (~26 employees, ~$1.1M rev) in Lewes, DE. Plausible family-owned Sussex County contractor consistent with Summit tuck-in profile if confirmed as active school-bus contractor; needs direct outreach to verify status, fleet size, ownership, and district contracts.

name: Cheyenne Bus Co LLC
districts_or_customers: Private events, weddings, tailgates, tours - NOT school district customers
recent_news: Founded 2012 when Tulsa attorney Dan Smolen and partners bought two 1978 Bristol VRT double-decker buses (formerly Minneapolis transit) and refurbished them (~$500K) into a charter/party-bus business named after the Cheyenne Ave warehouse where they're parked. FMCSA shows 2 power units, 1 driver, intrastate-only private passenger ops, NOT AUTHORIZED for interstate for-hire.
fit_notes: NOT A FIT - Not a school-bus operator. Two-vehicle vintage double-decker party/charter business in downtown Tulsa with no student transport contracts. Misclassified in source list; exclude from Summit tuck-in pipeline.

name: Spencers Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE - Molokai routes (Kawela to Kalamaula); listed in HI DOE Special Education Transportation manual
recent_news: Operates under Hawaii DOE 7-year school bus contracts with up to three 1-year extensions. One of three Molokai operators alongside Molokai Transportation Associates and Dudoit's Bus Service serving Molokai's single high school and middle school. Manager Betty Puaa testified to BOE in 2012 to preserve Molokai bus service from proposed cuts.
fit_notes: Genuine family-owned Molokai school-bus contractor with HI DOE contract revenues and SPED service - structurally on-profile but extremely small (~$1M rev, ~8 buses) and geographically remote (Molokai island). Hawaii is outside Summit's typical mainland footprint; tuck-in fit is weak unless Summit pursues an HI platform. Defensible local incumbent w/ limited competition on-island.

name: Pfeiler Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Anne Arundel County and/or Baltimore County Public Schools area (Linthicum Heights, MD) - specific district contracts not publicly confirmed
recent_news: Yellow Pages lists business as CLOSED, though other directories (Yelp, Nextdoor, CMac, BBB) still show it active; no recent news. Maryland uses a network of small private contractors via the Maryland School Bus Contractors Association.
fit_notes: Small family-owned Linthicum Heights (Anne Arundel County) contractor with founder Ronald Pfeiler - succession signal HIGH given 20+ year operation and owner-operator structure. Operating status uncertain (one source shows CLOSED); requires direct verification. If still active, MD is in-state for Summit and the contractor model fits Summit's tuck-in thesis.

name: School Bus Svc Of Ma
districts_or_customers: Likely Manatee County area private/charter schools or contract routes - unconfirmed. School District of Manatee County operates a large district-run fleet, so private contractor role would be limited (charter/private schools, SPED overflow, athletic/activity).
recent_news: No public news, website, or directory listing found. Name 'School Bus Svc Of Ma' likely abbreviates 'School Bus Service of Manatee' given Palmetto/Manatee County, FL address. Manatee County School District (district-operated buses) is the dominant public-school transport provider.
fit_notes: Very thin public footprint (20 employees, ~$839K rev, founded 2018) in Palmetto, FL (in-state for Summit and an expansion-state flag). FL has limited room for school-bus contractors because most FL districts self-operate, so likely serves private/charter schools or activity trips rather than yellow-bus route contracts - meaning it is borderline relevant for Summit's tuck-in thesis. Requires direct outreach to verify nature of operation.
